Name: Avalanche Ecosystem
Description: Avalanche is an open-source platform for the deployment of decentralized applications and multi-enterprise blockchains. Developed for fast, near-instant transactions, Avalanche is the first global smart contract platform designed for scale. As Solidity comes enabled out of the box, Ethereum developers can easily build on Avalanche. Avalanche's consensus protocol sets it apart from other decentralized platforms. Its decentralization remains intact even though it guarantees high levels of safety, speed, and finality with a novel consensus method. Among the most promising projects in its ecosystem is Wonderland, a long-term high yield stake farm and also a reserve currency of a metaverse.

Name: Cosmos Ecosystem
Description: The Cosmos ecosystem is a group of blockchain platforms that can scale and interact with each other. Until Cosmos, blockchains were standalone and uncoordinated, and they could only process a small number of transactions a second. Cosmos provides a solution to these problems using a new technological paradigm. Modular, flexible, and interchangeable tools form the foundation of the ecosystem. As blockchain technology becomes a reality, developers are encouraged to contribute their efforts to improving these existing tools and creating new ones. Its ecosystem includes coins such as Juno, OSMO, SCRT, and ATOM.

Name: Meme
Description: Meme coins are digital currencies inspired by internet jokes and, in particular, by the Doge meme. These coins typically start out completely ironically with no utility or inherent value as a way to remind the crypto community that pretty much anything can become a cryptocurrency. This idea, however, turns out to be valuable in and of itself, acting as a means of determining the difference between real value and speculative value. It moreover sheds light on how a market can move based on artificial influence and hype alone. Due to this, most of these coins experience large volatility spikes, and many people choose to invest in them. The most well-known meme coin is definitely Dogecoin. But there are many similar variants of the coin attempting to dethrone the meme king with similar concepts such as Shiba Inu or Dogelon Mars, which are in contrast ERC-20 tokens within the Ethereum ecosystem.

Name: Metaverse
Description: The Metaverse category is comprised of cryptocurrencies that fuel different metaverses. A metaverse is a shared virtual world where crypto can be used to buy and sell virtual assets such as land, buildings, avatars, and names. It is essentially an open-world game that includes a virtual marketplace where users can buy and sell virtual assets as well as meet friends. The name itself comes from the dystopian cyberpunk novel "Snow Crash" by Neal Stephenson and the concept can also be easily compared to many modern movies such as "Ready Player One".

Name: Near Ecosystem
Description: Thanks to Near Ecosystem, DeFi can now be accessed by everyone. Developers and end-users alike can enjoy one of the most user-friendly blockchain platforms in the world, while still receiving the scalability and security needed to serve their customers. Developers can host serverless apps and smart contracts on the NEAR Protocol, which is a decentralized development platform linked to open financial networks and fueled by an ecosystem of open web components. With NEAR, millions of people can finally create and discover new experiences thanks to simple, scalable, and secure technology. Near is responsible for the creation of prestigious projects such as Aurora or Ref Finance.

Name: Play to Earn
Description: Play to Earn (P2E) is the term used to describe crypto and blockchain projects related to the blockchain gaming sector. The idea behind the concept is to give players more power. In regular gaming, making a profit off video game characters and virtual items is often impossible or breaks the terms of service. The P2E model is here to change that and allow players to freely trade and truly own their items. By utilizing the NFT technology, players have complete ownership of their equipment and are free to earn money through their hobby. Some of the games that successfully adapted this idea are Axie Infinity and Decentraland, both of which let players utilize NFTs to earn off their time spent playing.

Name: Web 3.0
Description: Web 3.0 is the next evolution of the internet that utilizes the blockchain and tools of decentralization. In this next stage, websites and applications will be able to process the information on a human level. Users will possess complete ownership of their data and their activities unlike in Web 2.0. Although the new iteration of the world wide web isn't available yet, projects like Polkadot and Kusama are working hard towards realizing it.

Name: Terra Ecosystem
Description: Terra provides a thriving DeFi ecosystem and a suite of algorithmic decentralized stablecoins built upon a public blockchain protocol. The platform is built on Cosmos SDK, a framework that allows developers to build decentralized applications and custom blockchains. All crypto assets created by the Terra Protocol continuously track any fiat currency via the efficient combination of open-market arbitrage incentives and decentralized Oracle voting. Consequently, Terra users have access to instant stablecoin trading, exchanging, spending, or storing on the Terra blockchain. StarTerra, which is the core project in the ecosystem, offers Play2Earn, gamified NFTs, and multi-asset staking.

Name: Solana Ecosystem
Description: Solana's ecosystem consists of a number of technologies that work together to increase scalability and speed as well as a number of dApps that use its open infrastructure. A suite of developer resources and an open protocol make it easy for anyone to start building on the ecosystem. Solana currently boasts 50,000 transactions per second, making it the fastest blockchain in the world. Numerous developers and institutions choose to build on its ecosystem because of its high level of security, censorship resistance, and cost-efficiency. Some of the most promising projects on Solana include Kin, which is an SPL that is integrated on real apps such as Kik, Perfect365, MadLipz, and Tapatalk

Name: A2DAO IDO
Description: Since 2017, A2DAO IDO has been an investment platform which aims to raise funds for crypto startups as a means to accelerate the development of promising projects. Aside from this, the platform is also utilized to harbor a lively decentralized community where people can contribute to the future of cryptocurrency.

Name: AI
Description: AI (artificial intelligence) generally refers to computers performing complex tasks that typically require human intelligence. Cryptocurrencies falling under the AI category aims to use blockchain technology to achieve such tasks: analyzing data, self-learning ability, algorithms… For example, VeChain improves supply management and business procedures, while The Graph is a protocol for indexing and querying data from blockchains.

Name: Algorithmic Stablecoins
Description: Algorithmic stablecoins are cryptocurrencies pegged to a fiat currency. But instead of being backed by a reserve asset, these coins use stabilization measures like algorithms, rules and softwares to respond to market conditions, thus maintaining stability and circulating supply balance. Some examples of algorithmic stablecoins are Ampleforth and agEUR.

Name: AMM
Description: AMM stands for Automated Market Makers, which refers to the method of decentralized trading using algorithms to price assets in liquidity pools. AMM is popular in the DeFi space because of its automated nature, which centralized exchanges lack. The most popular AMMs include Uniswap and PancakeSwap.

Name: AscendEX Launchpad
Description: With AscendEX Launchpad, new crypto projects can raise money by granting early access to token sales to investors. AscendEX Launchpad belongs to AscendEX, a global platform for trading digital assets. Cryptocurrencies participating in the launchpad, such as Serum and Persistence are listed under this category.

Name: Backed Tokens
Description: Backed tokens basically refer to every token that has their value pegged to either another cryptocurrency or a real-world asset, be it fiat, gold, shares or other commodities. Examples include Marinade staked SOL, PAX Gold, Tether Gold…

Name: Balancer LBP
Description: Balancer LBP is a Liquidity Bootstrapping Pool by the decentralized exchange Balancer, used to enable decentralized trading and provide a liquidity pool. Users can issue tokens through the pool and the liquidity will be generated from a smaller seed amount. Unlike Uniswap, the weighting of both assets can change over time. For instance, some projects from this category are Illuvium and Perpetual Protocol.

Name: Big Data
Description: Big data is a term used to describe massive digital content that poses a challenge for traditional data management tools to handle. Different types of big data serve a variety of purposes, but they can all be powerful in discovering new potential markets. Projects under this category aim to integrate big data into blockchain projects, examples being Chainlink and 0x.

Name: Binance Launchpad
Description: Binance Launchpad was arguably the first integration of Initial Exchange Offering (IEO). It is a launch platform for promising, transformative blockchain projects. It ensures the quality of chosen projects by enforcing that the developers submit plenty of supportive information in their applications. Binance Launchpad claims to only list trusted projects to protect both users and developers. The most successful projects associated with Binance Launchpad are Axie Infinity and Polygon.

Name: Binance Launchpool
Description: Binance Launchpool rewards participants with new tokens after they have staked crypto assets to provide funds for a liquidity pool. This process is referred to as Yield Farming. Cryptos from this category include Klaytn and Mobox.

Name: Blockchain
Description: Blockchain, or distributed ledger technology, is a chain of blocks, each containing information. Then, the whole chain is distributed across a network of computers with the purpose of ensuring the blockchain is not managed by one centralized entity, thus safe from corruption. Many coins and projects have their own blockchain with different features, the most popular ones including Ethereum and BNB.

Name: Blockchain Service
Description: Coins in this category are designed to support the development, security and growth of blockchains. In general, blockchains services are protocols or solutions which increase the ease of interacting with crypto technology. For example, Render is a node distribution protocol while CertiK is a smart contract auditing service.

Name: Bounce IDO
Description: Bounce IDO is an Initial DEX Offering on the platform Bounce, which helps new projects to raise funds in a decentralized way. Some of the most notable projects under this categories are Dora Factory, O3 Swap and Antimatter.

Name: BSCPad IDO
Description: BSCPad IDO is a decentralized IDO platform to empower new crypto projects to raise money and distribute tokens. BSCPad IDO operates on the Binance Smart Chain Network, so people have to hold BSCPas tokens to participate. BSCPad differs from other IDO platforms in that it has a Tier system based on the number of token staked to guarantee an allocation spot. The platform has given rise to projects like AIOZ Network and StackOS.

Name: BTC
Description: The BTC category is reserved for cryptocurrencies that are considered variants of Bitcoin. These variants have their own purposes, such as building a bridge or transferring cryptocurrencies across multiple blockchains. For example, BTC Standard Hashrate Token and tBTC are both variants of Bitcoin.

Name: Cardano Ecosystem
Description: Cardano is a decentralized proof-of-stake blockchain that aims to be more efficient than its proof-of-work counterparts. The platform allows the use of smart contracts, which in turn enables the development of dApps, new tokens, blockchain games… There is also a secure voting system and consensus mechanisms that are scalable and fast. The Cardano ecosystem is home to cryptos like Cardano, Ergo, OccamFi and Paribus.

Name: Celo Ecosystem
Description: Celo is a proof-of-stake blockchain created to support stablecoins and tokenized assets. Celo uses an algorithmic reserve-backed stability mechanism to achieve this.

Name: CEX
Description: Cryptocurrency exchanges, or digital currency exchanges (DCEs) such as Binance Exchange and FTX, allow customers to exchange cryptocurrencies for other assets, such as conventional fiat money or other digital currencies. In exchange for digital currencies or cryptocurrencies, exchanges accept credit cards, wire transfers and other forms of payment. Cryptocurrency exchanges are usually market makers who take the bid-ask spreads as a commission for their services, or they may act as matching platforms that simply charge fees.

Name: Chromia Ecosystem
Description: The Chromia Blockchain Platform allows people to easily build decentralized applications in the real world. Previously, Chromia was known as Chromapolis. Chromia aims to become a blockchain platform that provides more benefits than Ethereum. Chromia is the centerpiece of the upcoming open-world game My Neighbor Alice, which is 100% blockchain-based and incorporates NFTs to represent in-game items, land parcels, and player avatars.

Name: Coinlist Launchpad
Description: CoinList allows you to be the first to find out about the best new cryptocurrency projects before the large exchanges. Traders of all levels can use CoinList. With CoinList.co, you can easily trade crypto assets such as BTC, ETH, and SOL as well as CoinList tokens such as CSPR, MINA, and FLOW. The world's largest Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) and Wrapped Filecoin (EFIL) merchant is CoinList. CoinList creates WBTC and EFIL instantly and deploy them on MakerDAO, dYdX, Curve, and other DeFi-compatible protocols. The FIL Lending Desk offers borrowers and storage miners a sustainable and reliable option for obtaining FIL loans with fixed terms or open terms.

Name: Cryptocurrency
Description: Cryptocurrency, cryptocurrency, crypto, or coin is a digital currency that operates as a medium of exchange through a computer network without any reliance on a central authority, such as a bank or government. Cryptocurrencies do not exist as physical objects (like paper money) and are not controlled by any central authority. In contrast to a central bank digital currency (CBDC), cryptocurrency uses decentralized control. Cryptocurrencies are tradable digital assets or digital forms of money that only exist online, building on blockchain technology. The name cryptocurrency comes from the fact that encryption protects and authenticates transactions. Currently, there are over 1000 cryptocurrencies available on the market. Bitcoin is the biggest cryptocurrency in the world, followed by Ethereum with its own Ecosystem.

Name: DAO
Description: In essence, Decentralized Autonomous Organization (DAO) operated as a venture capital firm, based on open-source code and lacks traditional management and governing structures. Since the DAO was completely decentralized, it was unaffiliated with any nation-state, though it utilized the Ethereum network. With DAO, human error or manipulation of investor funds is eliminated by placing decision-making power into the hands of an automated system and crowdsourcing the process. With DAO, Uniswap and Hedera has found success.

Name: DAO Maker IDO
Description: DAO Maker provides growth technologies to nascent and growing crypto startups through SaaS solutions. The company's flagship product is the DAO Pad, a multi-investment platform that enables holders of DAO tokens to participate in early-stage token sales for rigorously vetted upcoming projects. DAO Maker has launched several notable projects, such as My Neighbor Alice (ALICE), Orion Protocol (ORN), Seascape Network (CWS), and Infinity Pad (IPAD). DAO Maker offers a range of additional services to new blockchain-based projects in addition to its popular DAO Pad product.

Name: DeFi
Description: Decentralized Finance (DeFi or "decentralized finance") is a general term for financial services on public blockchains, primarily Ethereum. Most of what banks provide, including earning interest, borrowing, lending, buying insurance, and trading derivatives, as well as trading assets, can be done with DeFi, with fewer barriers and no paperwork. In addition to being open to all, DeFi is global, peer-to-peer (meaning it is not routed through a centralized system), anonymous, and open source.

Name: Derivatives
Description: Derivatives are contracts or products whose value is derived from underlying assets. A cryptocurrency token can be the underlying asset in crypto derivatives trading. Participants in a financial contract speculate about the price of a cryptocurrency in the future. There are both centralized (such as FTX) and decentralized platforms (such as Serum) for trading cryptocurrency derivatives. By using cryptocurrency derivatives exchanges, exchange owners can reach out to new investors. You can gain access to markets that you would not otherwise be able to access with a crypto derivatives trading platform.

Name: Dev Tooling
Description: Blockchain is rapidly evolving and has the potential to affect many different industries. Developers are coming up with innovative solutions in the areas of healthcare, insurance, shipping, finance, and governance. In many cases, these solutions are built utilizing decentralized applications that use smart contracts using Ethereum dApps, the most common blockchain development tools. The Ethereum blockchain is the most popular one among programmers and developers due to its capability to run smart contracts. Some of the most powerful tools run the Ethereum blockchain, including DAO Maker, with its native coin DAO, and The Graph, an indexing protocol for querying data.

Name: DEX
Description: Decentralized exchanges (DEX) are peer-to-peer marketplaces in which crypto traders can conduct transactions directly. In this way, DEXs bring one of crypto's most significant benefits: the ability to conduct financial transactions without the involvement of banks, brokers, payment processors, or other intermediaries. DEXs like Uniswap and Sushiswap, which use the Ethereum blockchain, are part of the growing suite of decentralized finance (DeFi) tools, making a variety of financial services available directly through compatible cryptocurrency wallets.

Name: DuckStarter IDO
Description: DuckStarter is a platform for token sales that supports Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain, and Polkadot networks. Instead of raising funds from funds, The Launchpad helps early-stage crypto projects such as Polkafoundry (PKF) and Dora Factory (DORA) raise capital from retail investors. Investors who participate in a project launch at an early stage are rewarded with early access to innovative product features.

Name: Elrond Ecosystem
Description: Elrond is a distributed protocol for transactional computation based on a sharded state architecture and a Proof of Stake consensus scheme with its own cryptocurrency, Elrond (EGLD). With Elrond, you can run the network on a standard computer, whereas most other blockchain networks require custom hardware and high energy consumption. Elrond's performance will scale up with the number of computers joining the network thanks to sharding, a method of parallelizing data and transaction processing. It will reach more than 100.000 transactions per second as the decentralized network grows. That is why some of the most scalable projects run on Elrond, including the decentralized blockchain infrastructure Aknr (AKNR) and Dusk Network (DUSK).

Name: Energy
Description: Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies consume a lot of energy, which negatively impacts the environment. In order to achieve a zero-carbon economy, companies like  WPP Energy GmbH and Energy Web Foundation (EWF) are aiming to produce electricity at a much lower cost and reduce landfill waste.

Name: Entertainment
Description: As of now, blockchain has proven to be an effective digital ledger when it comes to transparency, safety, and security. Blockchain-powered networks such as THETA can provide movies to users quickly and in high quality by using blockchain technology. Furthermore, with sprawling multiplayer universes like Decentraland, users can create, experience, and monetize content and applications.

Name: ETH Staking
Description: By contributing to the security of a blockchain, stakeholders can earn rewards. Proof-of-stake blockchains require that you own some of the coins in order to participate in mining and validating transactions. With Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency, investors have a popular option for long-term plans to invest in crypto, paving the way for successful synthetic derivative assets like Ankr Reward-Bearing Staked ETH (AETHC). ETH staking differs significantly from other cryptos in that you must commit your coins longer than other coins. Staking ETH is one way until the proof-of-stake system becomes operational. With Ethereum staking, your coins are tethered until the upgrade is complete, which could be 2023 or later.

Name: Ethereum Ecosystem
Description: Ethereum is a decentralized platform with its own blockchain, Ethereum (ETH). The meme coin, Shiba Inu (SHIB), is also built on Ethereum. Furthermore, DeFi, the collective term for financial products and services, is mainly available to users of this blockchain. Ethereum is an ecosystem in which anyone can create distributed applications (Dapps), smart contracts, and even own their own cryptocurrencies. These are all designed to be safe and reliable, and there is no need for middlemen like banks or agencies.

Name: Fan Token
Description: A Fan Token is a limited-production, finite digital asset whose ownership is recorded in a secure blockchain ledger. With Fan Tokens, you gain lifetime access to a private fan club with VIP rewards, promotions, games, and the opportunity to interact with other superfans. Token holders are also entitled to vote on team decisions and get other perks, such as signed gear. Most fan tokens, like the Manchester City Fan Token (CITY) and Juventus Fan Token (JUV) are run on the Chiliz blockchain.

Name: Fantom Ecosystem
Description: One of the biggest rivals of Ethereum is Fantom, a decentralized, permissionless, open-source layer-1 blockchain that enables dApps. The blockchain architecture is based on a consensus layer integrating several chains of execution. Fantom Foundation developed Lachesis as the base layer for its Lachesis protocol, Fantom's version of the Ethereum Virtual Machine. Aspects of the Proof-of-Work blockchain like transaction fees and slow transaction finality are improved by using DAG (Directed Acyclic Graph) technology.

Name: Finance
Description: A blockchain could streamline banking and lending services by reducing counterparty risk and decreasing issuance and settlement times. Some of the biggest platform specialized in finance are NEXO and the DeFi platform KAVA. By collating user activity and sanctioned data across a network, it allows for the authentication of documentation and the gathering of KYC/AML data, as well as credit prediction and scoring. Blockchain technology also automates syndicate formation, underwriting, and disbursement of funds, such as principal and interest payments, reducing cost, delay, and friction. As well as facilitating collateralization, digitization allows for real-time asset management, tracking, and enforcement of regulatory controls.

Name: FTX Launchpad
Description: FTX is a cryptocurrency exchange that offers crypto trading, options, volatility products, and leveraged tokens. Recently, it expanded its service offerings by adding Launchpad. FTX Launchpad is a platform where early-stage projects can raise funds by selling tokens for funds. Among the most well-known DEX are Serum (SRM) and Bonfida (FIDA), a full product suite that bridges the gap between Serum, Solana, and the users

Name: Gaming
Description: Blockchain plays a huge role in gaming because It is beneficial for game developers and entrepreneurs to use cryptocurrency for in-game purchases, as it provides a safe and secure environment. In gaming, blockchain enables players to create interoperable profiles of themselves and to buy and sell in-game assets securely. Blockchain gaming also allows players to store their in-game assets securely as well as own them fully, allowing them to have full control over their favorite games. Some of the most popular blockchain-powered games are Axie Infinity (AXS) and Decentraland (MANA).

Name: Gas
Description: On-chain gas usually refers to the cost associated with an on-chain transaction or a cost incurred during a transaction on the network. Different blockchains have different currencies they utilize as gas. During different conditions, usage costs can fluctuate up and down, but it mostly depends on how busy the network is. An entire blockchain therefore shares the same gas currency. For example, the Fantom network uses FTM as its on-chain gas and Solana uses SOL.

Name: Gate.io Launchpad
Description: The Gate.io Launchpad belongs to the Gate.io exchange, which is a crypto-trading platform founded in 2013. Gate.io is ranked among the top 10 crypto exchanges in the world based on trading volume. It specializes in offering assets with a high rate of return and superior quality. Through their Gate.io Launchpad, cryptocurrency projects can raise capital for their tokens and jump-start their careers. Besides their native currency (GT) the ecosystem hosts a number of great token in its ecosystem such as METIS or DAO.

Name: Gold
Description: Similar to stablecoins, gold-backed cryptocurrencies like PAX Gold and Tether Gold operate through digital tokens which are registered on the blockchain. A standard gold token is its value pegged to one troy ounce. Cryptocurrency holders thus gain benefits from both the physical attributes of gold and digital assets.

Name: Governance
Description: Tokens that represent voting power on a blockchain project are called governance tokens. Tokens play a significant role in maintaining the decentralization element in the DeFi sector. Owners of government tokens can vote and shape the future of their projects. For example, they can accept or decline a proposed improvement or even propose their own ideas.  Popular tokens include UNI, ANT, AAVE etc.

Name: Guilds
Description: Guilds are a community of blockchain players/gamers. These users collaborate and work together in different metaverses and play-to-earn games to achieve a common goal. Coins under this category include GamerCoin (GHX), a native utility token of the GamerHash platform, or GuildFi (GF) a Web 3 platform that is built to connect players, games, and investors

Name: Heco Ecosystem
Description: Huobi ECO Chain (also known as Heco) is an open-source Blockchain platform that is highly efficient and developed by the well-known Huobi exchange. HECO lets Ethereum developers quickly switch into developer mode, enabling them to easily integrate smart contracts. With its PoS mechanism, HECO is capable of handling over 500 transactions per second. In addition to Huobi's native token (HT), you can find other quality coins on their exchange, such as Mdex (MDX), an automated market maker for users and developers that enables a low slippage of trades and promotes high market depth and yields for market makers and liquidity providers.

Name: Huobi Launchpad
Description: With Huobi Launchpad, also known as Huobi Prime, users can order token allocations prior to the tokens appearing on the exchange. Users must hold Huobi Tokens (HT) in order to participate. The launchpad is owned by Huobi Global, a global provider of Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies.

Name: Ignition IDO
Description: Ignition, a PAID Network-based Initial DEX Offering (IDO) platform, offers an easy platform to swap tokens and grow the community at large. It is a decentralized exchange protocol designed to offer private and public swaps on the blockchain for Ethereum, Binance Smart Chain, and Polkadot.

Name: Infrastructure
Description: Infrastructure coins, in contrast to normal cryptocurrencies, serve the purpose of facilitating blockchain development and blockchain-related applications and encourage the growth of the sector. Such cryptos offer their services to traditional markets too, in some cases going beyond the cryptocurrency sphere. These include coins such as FTM or RUNE.

Name: Insurance
Description: NFTs are non-fungible tokens that represent a unique digital representation of a particular asset. These assets typically come in the form of pictures, GIFs, videos, or music. Even though NFTs have exploded in the art scene, they have many other applications, specifically in gaming and Play To Earn. This has been proven by games like Axie Infinity, The Sandbox, or Gods Unchained.

Name: IOT
Description: The Internet of Things (IOT) allows devices to connect to private blockchain networks to create tamper-proof records of shared transactions. You can share and access IoT data with your partners using IBM Blockchain - without relying on centralized management and control. All permissioned network members can verify each transaction to prevent disputes and build trust. The most striking projects in this category include IOTX, whose blockchain has been built from scratch using the innovative Roll-DPoS consensus system, or VET, Vechain's native token.

Name: Kucoin Launchpad
Description: The KuCoin exchange offers buyers and sellers the capability to trade and buy cryptocurrencies. Platform features include margin trading, futures trading, and peer-to-peer (P2P) trading. Customers also have the option of lending or staking their cryptocurrency to earn rewards. A new launchpad for projects on the KuCoin Smart Chain has also been launched on the exchange and is now called Kucoin Launchpad. Projects that are worthy of public backing can raise money by exchanging tokens for cash using this service. The ecosystem includes coins like Velo (VELO), a bridge asset linking traditional assets to digital credits, or Coti (COTI), a DAG protocol designed for building decentralized payment networks and stablecoins.

Name: Kusama Ecosystem
Description: Kusama is Polkadot's public pre-production environment, which allows anyone to experiment with and test any new blockchain or application before it is made public. Kusama therefore serves as a kind of sandbox for developers interested in testing early Polkadot projects. However, the crypto is actually traded publicly. Kusama is also used to test official Polkadot upgrades before they are released. The main projects in its ecosystem includes coins like Moonriver (MOVR), which provides a full Ethereum-like environment.

Name: Launchpad
Description: The purpose of launchpads is to provide crypto projects with a way of raising capital for their token through a platform that is already widely used. Among the most popular and successful launchpads in this category are crypto projects such as Raydium and Tenset. Projects that go through a launchpad are subjected to due diligence to ensure their authenticity and trustworthiness.

Name: Launchpool IDO
Description: Poolz IDO is a decentralized cross-chain Initial DEX Offering platform that empowers crypto startups to launch their coins via decentralized liquidity exchange. Built on Web 3.0 infrastructure, Poolz IDO helps projects raise money and investors to get early-staged access. Cryptos listed in this categories are associated with Poolz IDO, like Netvrk and SingularityDAO.

Name: Layer-2
Description: Layer 2 is a second protocol or framework layered over an existing blockchain network. Despite the fact that most major cryptocurrency networks are facing difficulties in scalability and speed, layer-2 tokens like Polygon aim to solve those problems.

Name: Lending Platform
Description: With lending platforms such as Aave or Maker, users can receive loans in liquid funds, such as stablecoins, without selling their virtual currency, by pledging their crypto as collateral. The collateral serves as a form of insurance. Users typically take out collateralized cryptocurrency loans either due to a need for quick liquidity or because they anticipate an increase in future cryptocurrency value.

Name: Leverage
Description: The term leverage refers to situations in which borrowed funds are used to finance an investment. Traders have to deposit stablecoins in order to open a leveraged position on a lending platform. By using leverage, a trader can increase his or her opening position size and magnify his or her initial deposit. In this way, the expected profit can be multiplied by a number of times. The situation may be reversed if the opposite occurs. If there are insufficient funds to cover the losses, your position may be liquidated. The main coins that fall under the leverage category are ETHBULL and ETH2X-FLI.

Name: Media
Description: The media category is comprised of crypto projects such as Theta or Tron and other blockchains that engage in communications through mediums such as news, movies, music, or promotional messages.

Name: Multi-Chain Platform
Description: Formerly known as Anyswap, the MultiChain blockchain platform provides the ability to create and deploy private blockchains within or among various organizations. In addition, financial transactions can be performed using the platform's API and command-line interface. Users can swap different coins through different mainnets. The platform supports over 25 different blockchains including mainnets such as Ethereum, Bitcoin, Terra, etc.

Name: Narnia
Description: Narnia is one of the largest Russian-speaking crypto communities on Telegram. The group is also known as Crypto Narnia and it is a speculative social community that shares anything from crypto-related analytics to memes. You'll find well-known coins in this category such as BNB, AVAX, or SOL.

Name: NFT
Description: NFTs are non-fungible tokens that represent a unique digital representation of a particular asset. These assets typically come in the form of pictures, GIFs, videos, or music. Even though NFTs have exploded in the art scene, they have many other applications, specifically in gaming and Play To Earn.

Name: Nodes
Description: ‘Node’ refers to a point of intersection in a network, or a computer connected to a chain of other computers. Thus, nodes are vital for the running of any blockchains. If a computer hosts and synchronises a copy of the entire blockchain, it’s called a ‘full node’.‘Node’ refers to a point of intersection in a network, or a computer connected to a chain of other computers. Thus, nodes are vital for the running of any blockchains. If a computer hosts and synchronises a copy of the entire blockchain, it’s called a ‘full node’. Some of the coins under this category are Horizen, Flux and Syscoin.

Name: Oracle
Description: Oracle is a tool that provides people with reliable data and information from the outside world like stock market prices. Oracle uses smart contracts to transfer these data onto the blockchain. This category is reserved for Oracle-related cryptocurrencies, such as Chainlink or Keep3rV1.

Name: PancakeSwap IDO
Description: PancakeSwap is a decentralized exchange, or an automated market maker (AMM) operating on Binance Smart Chain. PancakeSwap IDO is the platform’s Initial Dex Offering, used to help crypto projects to raise capital. Because of the decentralized nature of the platform, participating projects as well as investors benefit from fair trading and automatic liquidity. Examples of cryptos associated with PancakeSwap IDO are Hot Cross, Kalmar and Belt.

Name: Perpetual
Description: Perpetual is a protocol made with the goal of bringing democracy to powerful trading tools since leveraged derivatives trading used to be exclusive for centralized entities. Thus, Perpetual aims to provide these instruments for everyone. Coins related to Perpetual are listed in this category, such as Injective and FutureSwap, both being variants of a decentralized market.

Name: Polkadot Ecosystem
Description: Polkadot is famously referred to as the ‘blockchain of blockchains’. This is because it is a multichain used for connecting different blockchains into one network, each one working parallel with the others. Currently, the multichain consists of almost 100 blockchains, creating an unprecedented level of scalability and speed. For instance, some of the coins in the Polkadot Ecosystem are Moonriver, Origin Trail and RMRK.

Name: Polkastarter IDO
Description: Polkastarter IDO is the Initial DEX Offering of Polkadot, a platform to give exposure to new projects. Listed projects can raise funds and grow their audience while investors can get access to early-staged promising projects. To get whitelisted for the event, it’s required that participants hold a certain number of POLS. Polkastarter IDO has listed projects such as SuperFarm and Wilder World.

Name: Polygon Ecosystem
Description: Polygon is a platform designed for Ethereum scaling and infrastructure development. Polygon relies mainly on Polygon SDK, a flexible framework that enables building and connecting Secured Chains like Optimistic Rollups, zkRollups, Validium… and Standalone Chains like Polygon POS. The Polygon Ecosystem is a large one, including Polygon, Decentraland, Gala and many other cryptocurrencies.

Name: Poolz IDO
Description: Poolz IDO is a decentralized cross-chain Initial DEX Offering platform that empowers crypto startups to launch their coins via decentralized liquidity exchange. Built on Web 3.0 infrastructure, Poolz IDO helps projects raise money and investors to get early-staged access. Cryptos listed in this categories are associated with Poolz IDO, like Netvrk and SingularityDAO.

Name: Predictions
Description: The prediction market is like a smart-contract-powered version of betting. People can trade contracts whose prices are paid depending on a future event, such as who will win the next Super Bowl. The amount of the payout is based on how unpredictable the outcome of such events is. Some projects listed in this categories are Gnosis, Augur and Kleros.

Name: Privacy Coin
Description: Privacy coins, like the name suggests, aim to bring as much privacy as possible to the identity of the users. Although built using a public network, these coins can still hide the names, wallet addresses, balances and transaction amounts of the sender and receiver. They achieve this partly by mixing multiple transactions into one. The most famous privacy coins are Dash, Horizon and DigiByte.

Name: Protocol
Description: This is a category for coins with blockchain protocol technology, which greatly establishes the structures of the blockchain in a way that data has to follow a set of rules for it to be shared between computers. Examples of coins under the protocol category are Hedera, Elrond, and BitTorrent.

Name: Rebase Tokens
Description: A rebase token uses smart contracts to automate its circulating supply. So the supply can expand or contract depending on changes in token price. Cryptocurrencies related to Rebase include Olympus and Redacted Cartel.

Name: Social
Description: Social projects and applications such as Helium and Decentralized Social are interactive and communication-based. Their main aim is to serve as a medium for connecting creators with advertisers and publishers.

Name: Software
Description: This category includes projects such as Loopring or The Graph that are involved in the creation, implementation, or programming of crypto-related applications. This is an application, script, or program that runs on a device such as a PC, mobile phone, tablet, or other smart devices. Examples of blockchain-enabled software development include identity verification, workflow optimization, dev tooling, and data management.

Name: Sport
Description: This category covers all aspects of physical and mental wellness related to crypto projects and blockchains. For example, football clubs can issue tokens (fan tokens) in which owners can vote on the club's future, or a platform can sell NFT tokens.

Name: Stablecoin
Description: Stablecoins are cryptocurrencies that are backed by a reserve asset and aim to provide price stability. In recent years, stablecoins have gained popularity due to their ability to combine the benefits of cryptocurrency and fiat currency—instant processing and security of payments, and stable value without volatility.

Name: Storage
Description: Cryptocurrencies and blockchain projects in this category are protocols that provide incentives for users to provide unused hard disk storage space to the community. In comparison to centralized cloud storages, blockchain storage projects like BitTorrent (BTT) and Filecoin (FIL) are cheaper, safer, and more reliable.

Name: Wallet
Description: Here you can find projects and blockchains focusing on the development and application of crypto-wallet software. These projects aim to simplify wallet use and make them safer so they can handle multiple cryptocurrencies. Listed below are projects such as Cronos (CRO) and Elrond (EGLD) among other coins which fit this description.

Name: Wrapped Token
Description: The term "wrapped tokens" refers to tokens that are pegged to a particular cryptocurrency but are able to be used on other blockchain networks. Essentially, you can think of wrapped tokens as stablecoins' siblings. As an example, wrapped bitcoin, or WBTC, is derived from the BTC price but in addition, they're compatible with the Ethereum network. Other examples of wrapped coins are WCFG and WNXM.

Name: xDAI Ecosystem
Description: Ethereum sidechain xDai Chain offers low gas prices, 5-second block times, and its own native token, xDai which is stablecoin pegged to the dollar. xDai Chain is based on the delegated Proof-of-Stake architecture POSDAO, which was developed through a collaboration between POA Network and MakerDAO. This chain has the advantage of being extremely fast, extremely cheap, and requiring only one token (xDai) for transactions.

Name: Yearn Ecosystem
Description: Yearn.finance (YFI) is a group of protocols that runs on the Ethereum blockchain and enables users to lend and trade digital assets in order to maximize their earnings. Yearn products such as Sushi are all designed to provide a simple and intuitive interface to DeFi in general.

Name: Yield Aggregator
Description: Yield aggregator platforms such as Beefy.Finance and Convex Finance combine multiple farms in one place for the purpose of making farming easier. There are a lot of new farms popping up on the market, and moving back and forth from farm to farm can be tedious. Yield aggregators automate this process in order to optimize yield farming for their users.

Name: Yield Farming
Description: Yield farming refers to the practice of cryptocurrency traders investing in the DeFi market through exchange platforms such as Uniswap or PancakeSwap and earning a fixed or variable return. In order to become a liquidity provider, cryptocurrency holders need to lock up their crypto in a liquidity pool for a particular cryptocurrency pair. Participants in this market, such as traders, pay a certain exchange fee every time they trade one crypto for another. Those fees are then shared between liquidity providers. DeFi facilitates yield farming with smart contracts - a type of code that automates the process of financial agreements between participants. These smart contracts can be further modified into different variations such as yield aggregators

Name: Zendit IDO
Description: An IDO (Initial Dex Offering) allows promising projects to raise funding for their operations from the public via an online platform. Zendit is the IDO and product of MANTRA DAO, a community-governed DeFi platform. Zendit acts as a launchpad that distributes tokens fairly among members of the community. It has helped to raise funds for tokens such as SingularityDAO (SDAO) and Polkafoundry (PKF).

Name: Move To Earn
Description: Move-To-Earn (ME2) is the gamification of exercise similar to apps like Pokemon GO except in a classic crypto play-to-earn fashion (P2E). The concept is a combination of GameFi, SocialFi, and NFT. Projects like StepN, Green Satoshi Token, Genopets, etc., which provide cryptocurrency rewards rather than meaningless video game points to players who maintain a healthy lifestyle.
